prompt:
Swim club volunteer maintaining our heat-sheet generator here. Someone on the committee refactored the seeding code last night — the announcer screen now wants a slim `Seeded` row instead of the full entry, and the clerk screens got a chainable `EntryQuery` — and they left the build broken with a cheerful 'almost done!' in the group chat. They are on a plane. `dotnet test` output:

  HeatSheet.cs(57,41): error CS1061: 'Seeded' does not contain a definition for 'Club' and no accessible extension method 'Club' accepting a first argument of type 'Seeded' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
  HeatSheet.cs(57,59): error CS1061: 'Seeded' does not contain a definition for 'Age' and no accessible extension method 'Age' accepting a first argument of type 'Seeded' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
  HeatSheet.cs(63,38): error CS0266: Cannot implicitly convert type 'SwimHeats.EntryQuery' to 'System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<SwimHeats.Entry>'. An explicit conversion exists (are you missing a cast?)
  HeatSheet.cs(70,16): error CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<int, System.Collections.Generic.List<string>>' to 'System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, System.Collections.Generic.List<string>>' [SwimHeats.csproj]
  HeatSheet.cs(83,31): error CS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from 'System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<SwimHeats.Seeded>' to 'System.Collections.Generic.List<SwimHeats.Seeded>'

A couple of constraints from the committee, since Saturday's meet program prints from this: the announcer line must still show club and age, and the roster section is organized by the PRINTED age-group labels (10&U, 11-12, and so on), not raw ages — HeatSheetTests.cs is the authority on all of the output shapes. Please keep the new Seeded/EntryQuery design rather than reverting it; just finish the job. `dotnet test` fully green, no changes to HeatSheetTests.cs, SwimHeats.csproj, or .gitignore.
notes: 5C3 item 6: LINQ/type-inference triage, exactly 5 compile errors (dump in prompt captured verbatim from dotnet build on SDK 10.0.302). Planted: (a) var chain broken by a Select signature change — TopSeedLine projects to the new slim Seeded record then reads .Club/.Age which only Entry has (CS1061 x2; fix reads from the Entry before projecting, tests pin the announcer line so dropping club/age fails); (b) IEnumerable-vs-custom-query — chainable EntryQuery (deliberately IQueryable-ish: own Where returning EntryQuery, Items() to materialize) assigned to IEnumerable<Entry> (CS0266; fix materializes via Items(), and EntryQuery chaining itself is pinned by two tests so 'fixing' it by making EntryQuery implement IEnumerable-by-gutting still has to keep Where chainable); (c) group-by key type mismatch — GroupBy(e => e.Age) (int) flowing into Dictionary<string, List<string>> (CS0029; fix groups by the printed AgeGroup label, pinned by roster tests incl. within-group encounter order); (d) IEnumerable<Seeded> passed to List<Seeded> parameter after the LINQ rewrite (CS1503; fix .ToList()). Chain-reveal: none — all 5 report in round 1 and the suite is green after the four fixes (10/10). Edge teeth: empty-sheet TopSeedLine throws InvalidOperationException, Heats(0) throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException, empty Heats/JuniorCount pinned.
